title: API Routes
description: Type-safe API routes with Zod validation and OpenAPI generation

blocks.text: API routes are defined with defineApi, exported from howl.config.ts. The file system location determines the URL path automatically.
The URL is inferred from the file path. You can override it with the path field.
Declare a Zod schema in requestBody. Howl validates the JSON body before your handler runs. ctx.req.body is fully typed.

Howl no longer auto-redacts response fields. Earlier releases scrubbed any field literally named `password` from the response, but that gave a false sense of safety: `apiKey`, `token`, `secret`, `pwd`, `accessToken` etc. all leaked. Strip sensitive fields from the response object yourself before returning.
Declare a Zod schema in query. Values are parsed and validated. ctx.query() returns the typed object; ctx.query(key) returns a single value.
Set roles to require authentication. Howl calls checkPermissionStrategy from your config before the handler runs. An empty array means the route is public.
Add caching.ttl (seconds) to cache the response. Authenticated routes are cached per user; public routes are shared.

API handlers return a flat object: a `statusCode` field plus the response body fields. Howl wraps the body in `{ ok: true, data: ... }` automatically, merges middleware-set headers, and emits the response.

Earlier releases also accepted a nested `{ statusCode, data: {...} }` shape and unwrapped the inner `data` automatically. That dual-shape handling was removed — handlers must return the body fields flat alongside `statusCode`. If you need the wrapped format, return `{ statusCode, ...payload }` directly.

Errors thrown from a handler (or from the auth/validation pipeline) come back as `{ error, correlationId }`. The same id is set on the response as `X-Howl-Correlation-Id`, so a client report and the matching server log line line up.
The full route descriptor (DIR/NAME/METHOD) is logged server-side only. The wire payload no longer leaks internal naming, which used to give attackers a free fingerprinting channel.
Howl generates an OpenAPI 3.x spec from all registered APIs. Expose it on any route you choose.
